name: subagent-reports
title: Sub-Agent Reports
purpose: Typed, durable ADV sub-agent report ingestion and readback. Replaces ADV worker final-message fenced JSON with Zod-validated tool-call submission, sidecar report persistence, deterministic scope-aware dedupe, and machine-readable downstream consumers.

requirements.title: Typed tool-call report ingest
requirements.body: ADV worker reports MUST be submitted through adv_subagent_report_submit using strict Zod-validated payloads. Version 1 supports adv-engineer, adv-reviewer, adv-researcher, adv-tron, and adv-scanner-bundle payload variants, and rejects malformed or unsupported payloads before persistence.

scenarios.title: Malformed report rejected
given: A caller submits a report missing required fields
then: The tool returns INVALID_REPORT
No workflow signal is fired
No agenda follow-up is written

scenarios.title: Known optimized handoff agents validate structurally
given: A report uses agent adv-researcher, adv-tron, or adv-scanner-bundle with its required fields
then: The strict payload schema validates the agent-specific object
The report carries explicit source metadata
The workflow signal is fired only after schema validation succeeds

requirements.title: Signal-persisted sidecar report state
requirements.body: Accepted reports MUST persist via subagentReportSubmittedSignal into a change-level sidecar report store. Task-scoped adv-engineer and adv-reviewer reports keep legacy task.subagent_reports[] read compatibility, but new canonical persistence MUST be scope-aware sidecar storage. The workflow MUST dedupe repeated submissions by (change_id, scope, agent, attempt) using deterministic workflow state, and MUST map task-scoped report blockers into task error_recovery without defineUpdate-based mutations.

scenarios.title: Duplicate report key is idempotent
given: The sidecar already stores a report for change_id, scope, agent, and attempt
scenarios.when: The same report is submitted again
then: No duplicate sidecar report entry is appended
The workflow records the key in seenReportIds if needed
The tool response reports duplicate true

scenarios.title: Blockers feed error recovery
given: A submitted engineer blocker or reviewer blocking finding exists
then: The task error_recovery.last_error summarizes blocker location and issue
The error_recovery attempt records the report attempt number
No tool-layer heuristic owns persistence correctness

requirements.title: Queryable reports and consumers
requirements.body: adv_change_show include.subagentReports MUST return persisted sidecar report data with explicit source metadata. Legacy reports that still live on task.subagent_reports[] MUST remain readable through the same opt-in readback. Tool-layer consumers MUST process follow_ups[] into bounded agenda items and surface verification cross-check warnings without rolling back an already persisted report.

scenarios.title: Reports are queryable by change
given: A change has one or more persisted sidecar or legacy sub-agent reports
scenarios.when: adv_change_show is called with include.subagentReports true
then: The response includes _subagentReports
The response includes _subagentReportsMeta.total
The response preserves task.subagent_reports[] only for legacy/task compatibility

scenarios.title: Follow-ups become agenda items after persistence
then: The report signal is fired before agenda writes
Each follow-up is added with category subagent-followup and source metadata
Consumer failures are returned as warnings

requirements.title: No ADV worker fenced-report transport
requirements.body: ADV worker contracts MUST instruct persisted worker lanes to call adv_subagent_report_submit before exit. Apply, review, and harden context packets MUST include ATTEMPT so the report key is stable. Legacy TaskStructuredOutput extraction remains available for non-ADV callers but MUST short-circuit for tasks that already have persisted sub-agent reports.

scenarios.title: Worker contracts use tool-call transport
given: adv-engineer or adv-reviewer is spawned by ADV
then: The worker builds a schema-specific report object
The worker calls adv_subagent_report_submit with that object
The worker does not rely on final-message fenced JSON as the ADV report transport

scenarios.title: Legacy extraction skips persisted report tasks
scenarios.when: adv_task_checkpoint or compatible task completion sees legacy adv-output text
then: The completion signal omits structured_output extracted from the legacy text
Existing non-ADV structured_output behavior remains unchanged for tasks without subagent_reports

requirements.title: Schema-aligned worker context packets
requirements.body: ADV typed worker context packets MUST align with the strict report schemas that adv_subagent_report_submit validates. Task-scoped adv-engineer worker packets MUST include WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, TASK, and ATTEMPT anchors. Task-scoped adv-reviewer worker packets MUST include WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, TASK, PHASE, and ATTEMPT anchors. Change-scoped adv-researcher and adv-tron worker packets MUST include WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, SCOPE KEY, and ATTEMPT anchors. Orchestrator-submitted scanner bundle packets MUST include WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, SCOPE KEY, PHASE, and ATTEMPT anchors. Missing schema-derived identity anchors produce malformed reports and MUST remain INVALID_REPORT. Individual scanner lanes are separate non-persisted analysis workers and MUST NOT claim worker report transport or call adv_subagent_report_submit.

scenarios.title: Typed worker packets provide schema-derived identity
given: ADV spawns adv-engineer or adv-reviewer for implementation or remediation work
scenarios.when: The worker builds ENGINEER_REPORT or REVIEWER_REPORT for adv_subagent_report_submit
then: task-scoped packets provide WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, TASK, and ATTEMPT
change-scoped packets provide WORKING DIRECTORY, CHANGE, SCOPE KEY, and ATTEMPT
Missing TASK, SCOPE KEY, PHASE, or ATTEMPT is rejected as INVALID_REPORT instead of inferred heuristically

scenarios.title: Scanner lanes remain non-persisted
given: ADV spawns explore scanners during review or harden
scenarios.when: The scanner returns dimension-specific analysis JSON
then: The scanner packet is classified as scanner, not worker
The scanner is not instructed to call adv_subagent_report_submit
The scanner result is consumed by the orchestrator and is not persisted directly

requirements.title: Strict report scope model
requirements.body: Persisted reports MUST carry structural scope metadata. Task evidence reports use scope.kind task with task_id. Optimized handoff reports use scope.kind change with a structural scope_key. The schema MUST enforce agent/scope pairing: adv-engineer and adv-reviewer reports are task-scoped; adv-researcher, adv-tron, and adv-scanner-bundle reports are change-scoped.

scenarios.title: Invalid agent scope pair rejected
given: A report pairs adv-researcher with scope.kind task or adv-engineer with scope.kind change
then: The schema rejects the report as INVALID_REPORT
No tool-layer heuristic repairs the scope
No workflow signal is fired

scenarios.title: Source metadata preserved
given: A valid change-scoped report is submitted
scenarios.when: The report is read through include.subagentReports
then: The response includes agent, scope, workdir_used, attempt, and submitted_at source metadata
The response identifies whether the report came from sidecar or legacy task storage
The ordinary change/task read remains compact unless include.subagentReports is true

requirements.title: Optimized handoff variants
requirements.body: adv-researcher and adv-tron reports MUST persist compact optimized handoff fields instead of raw transcripts. adv-scanner-bundle reports MUST be orchestrator-submitted scanner bundle summaries; individual scanners keep no ADV tool access and cannot submit reports directly.

scenarios.title: Research and recon reports stay compact
given: A researcher or tron worker returns findings
then: The payload includes evidence, assessment, blockers or open questions, and recommended next action
Raw transcript persistence is not required
The sidecar stores the compact handoff

scenarios.title: Scanner bundle isolation
given: Review or harden scanners produce dimension-specific analysis
then: Only the orchestrator-submitted scanner bundle can be persisted
Individual scanners have no ADV tool access
The bundle records phase and scanner_count

requirements.title: Bounded follow-up agenda handling
requirements.body: Persisted report follow_ups[] MUST create bounded, source-tagged agenda items with category subagent-followup. Harden MUST inspect report-created agenda items and fix those that are safe, adjacent, and campsite/touched-scope applicable; non-applicable report-created agenda items MUST get rationale instead of silent ignore.

scenarios.title: Follow-up generation is bounded
given: A submitted report contains more follow_ups[] than the configured bound
then: Only the bounded number of follow-ups is written
Each item records report agent, scope, and attempt source metadata
The response warns when follow-ups are truncated

scenarios.title: Harden inspects report-created agenda
given: Report-created agenda items exist during harden
then: Safe adjacent campsite items are fixed
Non-applicable items receive rationale
Unrelated or unsafe work is not silently expanded into the release gate

requirements.title: Temporal replay and legacy compatibility
requirements.body: Sub-agent report persistence MUST preserve Temporal replay safety. Key-shape changes from legacy task_id keys to scope-aware sidecar keys MUST be versioned or compatibility-preserved. Existing adv-engineer and adv-reviewer behavior remains backward-compatible, including task/checkpoint consumers that detect legacy task.subagent_reports[].

scenarios.title: Legacy report history replays
given: A workflow history contains legacy task-scoped report submissions
scenarios.when: New workflow code replays the history
then: Temporal replay does not fail due to report key shape changes
Legacy task-scoped reports remain queryable
New sidecar reports use deterministic scope-aware keys

scenarios.title: Task/checkpoint compatibility remains intact
given: A task has either sidecar task-scoped reports or legacy task.subagent_reports[]
scenarios.when: Task completion and checkpoint consumers evaluate persisted reports
then: The consumer recognizes persisted report evidence from both sources
Existing adv-engineer and adv-reviewer report behavior stays backward-compatible
No duplicate legacy structured_output is extracted for already-reported tasks
